lesson.menu_desc: From the signed contract to the 100-day integration plan -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Block 2 -- Closing the Deal & Integration
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Vinicius Vale Bassan | Aula 11 -- Closing the Deal & Integration

listenings.text: Carlos, congratulations, the contract is signed and the deal is closed. Now the real work starts: integration. Let me brief you on the first hundred days. In week one we are going to announce the deal to both teams, because people are worried and they need a clear message. After that, we will run the onboarding for the new staff, and we are setting up joint teams for every key function. Retention is our biggest risk, so we are going to meet the top talent personally and keep them. The systems rollout starts in month two; it will be slow at first, then it speeds up. Each milestone has an owner and a date, and we are tracking them every week. The handover from the old leaders to the new ones happens at the end of the transition. If we manage the people well, the synergies will follow. Lead the integration the same way you led the deal: with a clear plan and a clear why.

Hi Carlos, this is Diana from the board. I wanted to leave you a quick recap after the integration kickoff today. Overall, the board is very pleased. The closing went smoothly, and the announcement to both teams was clear and calm, which is exactly what we needed. We like the hundred-day plan: the milestones are realistic and each one has an owner. Two things we want to watch closely. First, retention; we are going to lose people if the onboarding feels cold, so please keep it personal. Second, the systems rollout; it is the part most likely to slip, so build in some extra time. The handover of the regional leaders is the moment everyone will be watching, so prepare it carefully. Send me the updated timeline by Friday, and we will review progress at the end of the month. Great start, Carlos, and talk soon.
